skill.name: open-skills
skill.description: Interactive CLI for browsing, installing, synchronizing, and managing AI agent skills across Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Windsurf, Cline, and related editors.
skill.summary: Install and manage AI agent skills across multiple coding editors from one CLI.
skill.author: lumacoder

security_audit.summary: AI review confirms that many static findings are false positives from Markdown code fences, TypeScript import paths, regex.exec usage, lockfile package names, and normal CLI metadata. However, the package intentionally performs remote git/network operations and broad filesystem writes, and the developer server exposes unauthenticated registry mutation APIs with permissive CORS. No prompt injection attempt was found in the reviewed files.

high_findings.title: Unauthenticated Developer Registry Write API
high_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE. Dev mode starts a web server from --dev or OPEN_SKILLS_DEV and exposes POST /api/registry, which parses arbitrary JSON and writes it to registry/skills.json. The API uses Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * and server.listen(port), so a local browser or exposed interface could be used to alter the registry without authentication. Confidence: 0.88. Confidence reasoning: Direct code evidence shows unauthenticated write behavior and permissive CORS; exploitability depends on dev mode being active and reachable.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct code evidence shows unauthenticated write behavior and permissive CORS. Exploitability depends on dev mode being active and reachable.

high_findings.title: Remote Git Content Installed Into Editor Skill Directories
high_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE. Install and sync flows clone URLs from registry metadata and copy or transform the resulting SKILL.md content into editor-specific targets. This is expected functionality, but it is high risk for a marketplace skill because unreviewed remote skill text can influence AI assistants after installation. Confidence: 0.82. Confidence reasoning: The implementation directly clones registry-controlled URLs and writes transformed content to AI editor targets; malicious impact depends on registry content and user selection.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The implementation directly clones registry-controlled URLs and writes transformed content to AI editor targets. Malicious impact depends on registry content and user selection.

medium_findings.title: Managed Directory Cleanup Can Remove Existing Files
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE. Directory-mode installs call cleanDirectory before generating output and remove files not in the expected managed set. This behavior is documented, but it can cause data loss inside editor skill directories if users store manual files there. Confidence: 0.78. Confidence reasoning: The deletion logic is explicit and reachable during directory adapter installs; the blast radius is limited to computed editor target directories.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The deletion logic is explicit and reachable during directory adapter installs. The blast radius is limited to computed editor target directories.

medium_findings.title: Developer Panel Renders Registry Values With innerHTML
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE. The web panel inserts registry category, skill, description, and tag values into innerHTML without escaping. If a registry entry contains markup, the local dev panel can execute injected script in the browser context. Confidence: 0.76. Confidence reasoning: The sink is direct innerHTML from registry-controlled data; the context is a developer-only panel, which lowers but does not remove risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The sink is direct innerHTML from registry-controlled data. The context is a developer-only panel, which lowers but does not remove risk.

medium_findings.title: Network Fetches to GitHub and SkillStore Are Intended But Trust-Sensitive
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE. The resolver and search utilities fetch metadata from GitHub and SkillStore based on user or registry input. This is core functionality, not exfiltration, but it expands trust and availability boundaries. Confidence: 0.70. Confidence reasoning: Network calls are explicit and limited to public metadata endpoints; no credential transmission was found.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network calls are explicit and limited to public metadata endpoints. No credential transmission was found.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are Mostly Documentation or API Name False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. Many Ruby backtick and command execution hits occur in Markdown examples, TypeScript import paths, or names such as npm scripts. The concrete marker-system hit is RegExp.exec, not process execution. Confidence: 0.91. Confidence reasoning: Reviewed examples show documentation code fences or JavaScript regular expression execution rather than shell execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed examples show documentation code fences or JavaScript regular expression execution rather than shell execution.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Are Identifier False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reported weak cryptography hits map to words such as hash, slug, license, or package metadata rather than MD5, SHA1, DES, or custom cryptographic use. No evidence found of cryptographic decision logic in reviewed implementation files. Confidence: 0.84. Confidence reasoning: Reviewed cited files use metadata, registry fields, and string transformations; no weak cryptographic primitive was identified.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed cited files use metadata, registry fields, and string transformations. No weak cryptographic primitive was identified.

low_findings.title: Prompt Injection Search Found No Override Instructions
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. Targeted review found no text telling the evaluator to ignore instructions, skip security analysis, claim pre-approval, or override the audit process. Ordinary TypeScript override keywords were present in adapter classes only. Confidence: 0.86. Confidence reasoning: Targeted semantic checks found only normal code keywords and no instruction-like attack text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted semantic checks found only normal code keywords and no instruction-like attack text.

low_findings.title: Create Command Validates Interactive Skill Names But Not Positional Names
low_findings.description: NEEDS_REVIEW. The interactive create flow validates names with a lowercase slug regex, but positional command-line names bypass that validation before path construction. This is a local developer command, so the practical risk is limited. Confidence: 0.62. Confidence reasoning: The code validates only prompt input and then joins the resulting name into bundles/skills; user intent is required to trigger it.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code validates only prompt input and then joins the resulting name into bundles/skills. User intent is required to trigger it.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Plus Filesystem Plus AI Instruction Installation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill combines remote fetching, git clone, filesystem writes, and AI editor configuration updates. This combination is expected for the product, but it requires strong source review and safer defaults before publication. Confidence: 0.83. Confidence reasoning: The pattern is semantically confirmed across install, sync, engine, and adapter code paths.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is semantically confirmed across install, sync, engine, and adapter code paths.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unauthenticated Local Web Mutation Surface
dangerous_patterns.description: The developer server exposes state-changing API routes without authentication or origin restrictions. This is dangerous if the server is reachable by untrusted browser content or network clients. Confidence: 0.86. Confidence reasoning: CORS, request handling, and registry writes are all visible in the cited implementation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: CORS, request handling, and registry writes are all visible in the cited implementation.

content.value_statement: Teams often maintain separate skill files for each AI coding editor. open-skills centralizes discovery, installation, synchronization, and registry management from one command-line workflow.
actual_capabilities: Starts an interactive CLI for selecting editors, install scope, categories, and skills.
Installs selected skills into Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Cline, Roo-Cline, Hermes, Antigravity, and GitHub Copilot targets.
Lists, searches, validates, exports, and imports skill registry data.
Synchronizes selected GitHub or git-backed skills into local bundles using shallow clone and sparse checkout.
Provides a development panel for creating skill scaffolds and editing registry metadata.
Transforms skill content into editor-specific directory or rule-file formats.
limitations: Remote SkillStore origins are not fully installable through the legacy installer path without a git URL.
Directory-mode installs clean unmanaged files inside target skill folders.
Remote search and synchronization depend on GitHub, SkillStore, network access, and local git availability.
The developer web panel is intended for local development and should not be exposed to untrusted networks.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team AI Editor Setup
use_cases.description: Install the same approved skills into several AI coding editors so contributors share consistent assistant behavior.

use_cases.title: Maintain a Private Skill Registry
use_cases.description: Create, validate, export, import, and synchronize a curated registry of reusable skills for internal workflows.

use_cases.title: Test Skills Across Editor Formats
use_cases.description: Generate editor-specific outputs for Claude Code, Cursor, Cline, Windsurf, and related tools from one skill source.

prompt_templates.title: Install Selected Skills
prompt_templates.prompt: Use open-skills to install frontend and devops skills into Claude Code and Cursor for this workspace. Explain each prompt choice before proceeding.

prompt_templates.title: Find Relevant Skills
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the open-skills registry for skills related to testing and API design. Summarize the best matches and the editors they can target.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Local Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new local skill scaffold named api-review-checklist in the backend category, then explain which files were created and how to validate it.

prompt_templates.title: Audit and Synchronize Registry Sources
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the registry for remote git or GitHub origins, identify which skills can be synchronized, then run the safest sync plan for one selected skill.

output_examples.input: Install two frontend skills into Claude Code for the current project.
output: The assistant opens the editor, scope, category, and skill selection flow.
It writes selected skills under the Claude Code skills directory for the selected scope.
It reports which skills succeeded and which failed.

output_examples.input: Export my current AI skill setup.
output: The assistant detects installed directory-mode skills for supported editors.
It writes a stack file that can be imported later.
It explains that rule-file editors are not detected by the export command.

output_examples.input: Create and validate a new skill scaffold.
output: The assistant creates a local bundle with a starter SKILL.md file.
It registers the skill metadata in the registry.
It runs registry validation and reports any missing fields.

best_practices: Review remote skill content before installing it into global editor directories.
Use local scope first when testing new skills or registry changes.
Back up existing editor rule files and skill directories before running install, update, sync, or import commands.
anti_patterns: Do not run the developer web panel on a shared or exposed network interface.
Do not install unreviewed remote skills directly into global Claude Code or editor directories.
Do not keep personal files inside managed editor skill directories because cleanup may remove unmanaged entries.

faq.question: What editors does open-skills support?
faq.answer: It targets Claude Code, Hermes, Cursor, Windsurf, Cline, Cursor Skills, Roo-Cline, Antigravity, and GitHub Copilot.

faq.question: Does it download remote content?
faq.answer: Yes. Search, resolver, sync, and install paths can fetch GitHub, SkillStore, or git-backed skill sources.

faq.question: Can it overwrite existing editor files?
faq.answer: It refuses unmanaged rule files for some adapters, but directory-mode installs clean files not in the expected managed set.

faq.question: Is the developer web panel safe for production use?
faq.answer: No. It is a local development tool and exposes unauthenticated registry APIs when started.

faq.question: Can it create new skills?
faq.answer: Yes. The create command and developer panel generate starter SKILL.md files and registry entries.

faq.question: Does it work with Codex?
faq.answer: The marketplace metadata marks Codex support, and the CLI can manage shared AI agent skill content for Codex-adjacent workflows.
